narrative.headline: What 2015 Kia Soul owners report most
narrative.summary: Engine problems dominate owner complaints for this model year, with reports centering on unexpected power loss and stalling, particularly tied to the stop-start system. Electrical system and fuel/propulsion complaints follow, frequently describing the vehicle failing to accelerate or respond when the pedal is depressed. Powertrain reports round out the picture, with owners noting sudden loss of power at speed with no warning.

issues.sample: CAR IS EQUIPPED WITH ECO-BOOST AND ACTIVE "INTERMITTANT STOP AND GO" SYSTEM, WHICH IS ACTIVE EVERY START CYCLE UNLESS "DEACTIVATE" BUTTON IS ENGAGED.CAR HAS BEEN DRIVEN BY 18 YEAR OLD DAUGHTER FOR 3,000 MILES IN 6 MONTHS. THE ENGINE HAS SHUT DOWN AND NOT BEEN ABLE TO BE RESTARTED
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report problems with the automatic stop-start system, which reactivates at every ignition cycle and requires manual deactivation each time the vehicle is started. · 58 fire · 4 crash · 9 injured

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle failing to accelerate when the pedal is depressed while stopped, accompanied by a loud noise, requiring the engine to be shut off and restarted. · 8 fire · 1 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: Owners report a pronounced lag in acceleration when the automatic transmission shifts between lower gears, describing the delay as a hazardous condition when pulling into traffic. · 6 fire · 3 crash · 5 injured

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle suddenly losing power without warning while the accelerator pedal is depressed at moderate speeds, with dealers unable to reproduce or diagnose the failure. · 8 fire · 1 crash · 3 injured
